title: External nose
body_html:
Arterial supply

The RCEM expect candidates to know that the external nose receives arterial supply from branches of the maxillary and ophthalmic arteries.

There is some additional arterial supply to the septum and alar cartilage via branches of the facial artery (angular artery and lateral nasal artery) but this is beyond the expected knowledge of the syllabus.

Innervation

Sensory innervation to the external nose is from branches of the ophthalmic and maxillary divisions of the trigeminal nerve (V1 and V2). The diagrams and table below outline the distribution.



Cutaneous sensation to the external nose is via the following

NerveOriginArea supplied
Infratrochlear nerveBranch of nasociliary nerve.
Ophthalmic branch of trigeminal (V1)		Bridge of the nose
External nasal branches of anterior ethmoidal nerveContinuation of nasociliary nerve.
Ophthalmic branch of trigeminal (V1)		Lateral superior sides of the nose to tip
Infraorbital nerveMaxillary branch of trigeminal (V2)Lateral lower half of nose including ala
